summary:
The Waldeyer’s ring includes the nasopharyngeal, palatine, tubal, and lingual tonsils forming a protective barrier at the upper respiratory and digestive tracts' entrances. Post-auricular nodes are not part of this structure as they drain the scalp/temporal area and are unrelated to the oral or nasal passages’ immune surveillance.

Note that you must only summarize what was said about the patient case; no external information should be added.
